final_answer:
Routed the Google Trends learning frontier through the human-gated self-improvement loop: of 80 trending prompts, the engine already resolves 80 and leaves 0 on the learning frontier. Every frontier trace was handed to the issue-#558 learner, which — because trending searches are open-domain questions, not program-plan modifiers — proposed 0 rules and adopted 0: nothing changes without human review. The report is a pure function of the committed catalog.

Generated document (google-trends-learning.lino):

google_trends_learning
  record_type "google_trends_learning_report"
  issue "498"
  auto_learning_loop "issue_558_self_improvement"
  human_gated "true"
  summary "The trending-prompt frontier is empty: every catalog prompt now routes through the engine. Issue #701's learning cycle closed the gap by deriving the request-opener surfaces in `data/seed/learned-request-openers.lino` from the frozen frontier record (`data/meta/learning-frontier-google-trends.lino`) and promoting them through the human-gated issue-#656 protocol; the per-prompt capability deltas are recorded in `data/meta/learning-adoption-ledger.lino`. Nothing is auto-adopted here."
  total_prompts "80"
  handled_by_engine "80"
  learning_frontier "0"
  learning_run_id "self_improvement_run_6ead622b844d76ae"
  learning_run_trace_count "0"
  learning_run_proposals "0"
  learning_run_adopted "0"
  learning_run_rejection_reason "none"
  benchmark_suite "issue_362_multilingual_coding_modification"
